_comment: Top-level acceptance check for the whole goal. MUST start RED (fail before the feature exists) so completion can be proven failure-first. A bare npm test or pnpm build exits 0 on an empty scaffold -- the driver will reject such vacuous gates. Gate on CONTENT: run a specific test that asserts your real goal is met, or a verify script that checks the built artifact contains the expected output.
_negative_assertion_tip: Strong done-checks also encode ABSENCE: the legacy thing is GONE, not just the new thing present. For surface migrations, copy dist/ralph/surface-scan-template.mjs and add a forbiddenPatterns list to assert no legacy signatures remain. Start it RED (offenders listed) before migration; green means the surface is clean. See issue #13 for the root-cause scenario.
